Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. OpenBSD on SGI: A Rollercoaster Story 64 upvotes, 2 comments
  2. Show HN: Jido 2.0, Elixir Agent Framework 72 upvotes, 10 comments
  3. Wikipedia in read-only mode following mass admin account compromise 54 upvotes, 9 comments
  4. Google Workspace CLI 801 upvotes, 261 comments
  5. Building a new Flash 647 upvotes, 211 comments
  6. Jails for NetBSD – Kernel Enforced Isolation and Native Resource Control 75 upvotes, 17 comments
  7. Nvidia PersonaPlex 7B on Apple Silicon: Full-Duplex Speech-to-Speech in Swift 281 upvotes, 93 comments
  8. Arabic document from 17th-cent. rubbish heap confirms semi-legendary Nubian king 109 upvotes, 33 comments
  9. Something is afoot in the land of Qwen 751 upvotes, 322 comments
  10. Google Safe Browsing missed 84% of phishing sites we found in February 126 upvotes, 44 comments
  11. Poor Man's Polaroid 126 upvotes, 45 comments
  12. Smalltalk's Browser: Unbeatable, yet Not Enough 107 upvotes, 38 comments
  13. World-first gigabit laser link between aircraft and geostationary satellite 95 upvotes, 37 comments
  14. Earth Garden: Field Recordings Around the World 35 upvotes, 10 comments
  15. Intelligence is a commodity. Context is the real AI Moat 53 upvotes, 18 comments
  16. Greg Kroah-Hartman Stretches Support Periods for Key Linux LTS Kernels 9 upvotes, 1 comments
  17. Good software knows when to stop 96 upvotes, 48 comments
  18. Fast-Servers 39 upvotes, 16 comments
  19. The L in "LLM" Stands for Lying 524 upvotes, 342 comments
  20. No right to relicense this project 392 upvotes, 255 comments
  21. Judge Orders Government to Begin Refunding More Than $130B in Tariffs 542 upvotes, 408 comments
  22. The Man Who Broke into Jail 32 upvotes, 16 comments
  23. The IRIX 6.5.7M (sgi) source code 23 upvotes, 11 comments
  24. Unix Isn't for Agents 15 upvotes, 6 comments
  25. AMD will bring its “Ryzen AI” processors to standard desktop PCs for first time 172 upvotes, 150 comments
  26. Relicensing with AI-Assisted Rewrite 296 upvotes, 300 comments
  27. Billy bookshelves as a retro motherboard "rack" 44 upvotes, 34 comments
  28. MacBook Neo 1880 upvotes, 2180 comments
  29. BMW Group to deploy humanoid robots in production in Germany for the first time 200 upvotes, 214 comments
  30. US tech firms pledge at White House to bear costs of energy for datacenters 141 upvotes, 170 comments

Last generated 1:00am, 6 March 2026. Source here.
